data.title: Large USDT Transfer to Aave Raises Market Interest
data.description: A significant transfer of 290 million USDT was made from an unidentified wallet to Aave, valued at approximately $290 million. According to NS3.AI, this transaction has captured the attention of market participants, sparking discussions about potential impacts on liquidity and market dynamics. The movement of such a substantial amount of USDT into Aave could influence lending rates and borrowing activities within the platform, as well as broader market sentiment. Observers are closely monitoring the situation to assess any further developments or shifts in investor behavior.

data.title: European Coalition Offers Military Support for Strait of Hormuz Security
data.description: A European-led coalition has proposed military assistance to ensure safe passage through the Strait of Hormuz. Bloomberg posted on X that this offer comes shortly after Iran announced its intention to open the vital waterway. Meanwhile, U.S. President Donald Trump has cautioned NATO members to maintain their distance from the situation.

data.title: IMF Chief Warns of Rising Daily Risks Impacting Growth and Inflation
data.description: International Monetary Fund (IMF) Managing Director Kristalina Georgieva has expressed concerns about the increasing risks faced on a daily basis. According to Jin10, Georgieva highlighted the potential for these risks to lead to a scenario characterized by lower economic growth and higher inflation. Her remarks underscore the challenges that global economies may encounter as they navigate these uncertainties.

data.title: Binance Life's market capitalization surpasses $410 million, setting a new high in its rebound.
data.description: According to GMGN data, Binance Life's market capitalization has surpassed $410 million, setting a new high for the rebound, currently trading at $400 million, with a daily increase of over 13%. Odaily reminds users that the price of Meme coin is highly volatile, and investors should participate with caution.

data.title: RAVE broke through $22.66, hitting a new all-time high, with a daily increase of over 35%.
data.description: OKX data shows that RAVE has broken through $22.66, setting a new all-time high, and is currently trading at $22, with a daily increase of over 35%.

data.title: Federal Reserve's Daly Comments on Future Interest Rate Path
data.description: Federal Reserve official Daly has stated that the future trajectory of interest rates will depend on the duration of ongoing conflicts. According to ChainCatcher, Daly mentioned that if the conflicts conclude swiftly, the policy will revert to the previous interest rate path.

data.title: Inside the Coffee Grading Room: Setting Global Standards
data.description: Wall Street Journal (Markets) posted on X, offering a glimpse into the unique process of coffee grading that sets global benchmarks. Expert graders meticulously evaluate coffee by sniffing, slurping, and spitting to determine its quality. This rigorous process ensures consistency and high standards in the coffee industry, impacting prices and consumer choices worldwide. The graders' expertise plays a crucial role in maintaining the integrity of coffee quality, influencing market dynamics and consumer preferences.

data.title: Fed's Daly: Interest rates may remain unchanged
data.description: Federal Reserve Chairman Daly indicated that the Fed may keep interest rates unchanged; if inflation accelerates, rates will need to be raised; and if the conflict ends quickly, rates may be cut. Maintaining the current policy will continue to curb inflation, medium- to long-term inflation expectations are stable, and as bank capital requirements change, reserve demand may decrease, potentially shrinking the Fed's balance sheet. (Jinshi)

data.title: The NYSE has submitted a proposal to amend its rules to allow tokenized securities to be listed and traded.
data.description: The New York Stock Exchange (NYSE) has submitted a rule amendment proposal to the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC), proposing a new Rule 7.50 and revising related provisions to allow eligible securities to be traded on the exchange in tokenized form. This proposal builds upon the Depository Trust Company (DTC) three-year tokenization pilot program and is modeled after similar rules previously approved by the SEC for Nasdaq. According to the proposal, tokenized securities must share the same CUSIP number, trading symbol, and shareholder rights as traditional securities to be traded with equal priority in the same order book. Initially, the application will be limited to Russell 1000 index constituents and ETFs tracking major indices, with the settlement cycle remaining T+1. Existing regulatory rules will also apply to tokenized securities.

data.title: Quantum Blockchain Raises Funds for AI-Driven Bitcoin Mining
data.description: Quantum Blockchain, a UK-listed company, has announced a placement of 142,857,142 new shares at a price of 0.35 pence per share, raising £500,000. According to Odaily, the company also revealed the establishment of a new Bitcoin mining subsidiary, BlocKeeper. The newly acquired funds will be directed towards accelerating the development of its AI-driven Bitcoin mining technology.

data.title: ORDI Experiences Significant Price Drop
data.description: ORDI's price briefly fell below 6 USDT, reaching a low of 5.818 USDT, according to Foresight News. It is currently reported at 6.077 USDT, marking a decline of approximately 25.74% over the past 24 hours.

data.title: Federal Reserve's Daly Comments on Current Federal Funds Rate
data.description: The Federal Reserve's Daly has noted that the current federal funds rate is slightly restrictive, positioned just above the neutral level of 3%. According to ChainCatcher, this assessment reflects the ongoing monetary policy stance aimed at managing economic conditions.

data.title: NYSE Proposes Rule Change for Tokenized Securities Trading
data.description:
The New York Stock Exchange (NYSE) has submitted a proposal to the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) to amend its rules, aiming to introduce Rule 7.50. According to Foresight News, this rule would allow eligible member institutions to trade tokenized securities within the framework of the DTC's three-year tokenization pilot program. This move aligns with a similar rule change by Nasdaq, which received SEC approval on March 18.

The proposal specifies that the scope of tokenized securities trading will be limited to components of the Russell 1000 Index and ETFs tracking major indices. Tokenized securities must share the same CUSIP and trading codes as traditional securities and grant holders the same rights. This ensures they can be traded alongside traditional securities on the same order book, with unchanged execution priority rules and settlement standards remaining at T+1.

The NYSE emphasized that all existing regulatory rules will apply equally to tokenized securities, including short-selling regulations, risk management, and market surveillance mechanisms. The overall framework does not require significant exemptions or a parallel market structure.

data.title: U.S. President Trump Declares Iran Funds Will Remain Frozen
data.description: U.S. President Donald Trump announced that the United States will not release the frozen Iranian funds. According to Odaily, Iran has agreed to indefinitely suspend its nuclear program.

data.title: U.S. President Trump Vows to Maintain Hormuz Strait Blockade
data.description: U.S. President Donald Trump announced on April 17 that the United States will continue its blockade of the Hormuz Strait until an agreement is reached with Iran. According to BlockBeats, this statement follows threats from Iranian officials who warned that if the U.S. Navy's blockade persists, Tehran will consider it a violation of the ceasefire agreement and may close the Hormuz Strait.

data.title: Trump: There may be more negotiations on the Iran deal this weekend, and I may travel to Islamabad.
data.description: US President Trump stated that he might travel to Islamabad once an agreement is reached, but no decision has been made yet. More negotiations regarding an Iran deal are expected this weekend. The US will continue to block the Strait of Hormuz until an agreement is reached with Iran. The US will cooperate with Iran to recover its enriched uranium and repatriate it to the US. There will be no monetary exchange in the potential deal with Iran; the US is cooperating with Iran to clear mines from the Strait of Hormuz. (Jinshi)

data.title: Trump: The US will not release frozen Iranian funds.
data.description: Trump stated that the United States will not release frozen Iranian funds, and Iran has agreed to indefinitely suspend its nuclear program. (Jinshi)

data.title: Today, US Bitcoin ETFs saw a net outflow of 142 BTC, while Ethereum ETFs saw a net inflow of 22,357 ETH.
data.description: According to Lookonchain monitoring, today the US Bitcoin ETF saw a net outflow of 142 BTC, the Ethereum ETF saw a net inflow of 22,357 ETH, and the Solana ETF saw a net inflow of 1,828,328 SOL.

data.title: Abby Johnson's Journey to Leading Fidelity
data.description: Abby Johnson, now at the helm of Fidelity Investments, faced significant challenges before taking over one of Wall Street's most prominent dynasties. Wall Street Journal (Markets) posted on X that Johnson had to navigate skepticism not only from her competitors but also from her father. Her journey to leadership involved overcoming these doubts and proving her capabilities in a highly competitive financial industry. Johnson's story is a testament to her resilience and determination in the face of adversity.

data.title: Iran has set conditions for passage through the Strait of Hormuz: passage must be via designated channels, and military vessels are prohibited from passing through.
data.description: Iran's Tasnim News Agency reported on the 17th, citing an informed source, that Iran has set three conditions for ship passage through the Strait of Hormuz: First, only merchant vessels can pass; military vessels are prohibited, and the vessels and their cargo must not be associated with hostile countries. Second, vessels must use waterways designated by Iran. Third, passage must be coordinated with the Iranian Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps Navy. The source also said that if the United States continues its maritime blockade, Iran will consider it a violation of the ceasefire agreement and close the Strait of Hormuz. (Xinhua)
